description: Correct the current active Discord linkage shape by placing active Discord identity on profiles while preserving historical connection records separately.

items.description: Inventory the current Discord linkage reality across profiles, oauth connections, migrations, metadata registration, and tests before changing linkage placement.
steps: Search the repo for existing Discord/profile/integration fields, models, migrations, vocabulary helpers, and tests.
Confirm whether the current active Discord linkage still lives primarily in oauth_connections, whether profiles only carries discord_username today, and whether a history table already exists.
Record the exact metadata-registration, Alembic-discovery, and compose-test wiring before editing.

items.description: Add or align active Discord linkage fields on profiles (`discord_user_id`, `discord_username`, `discord_integration_status`) using the approved vocabulary and one-model-per-file structure.
steps: Update the profile model and migration(s) so the current active Discord linkage lives on profiles.
Use the approved integration status vocabulary.
Do not add extra Discord fields outside approved scope.

items.description: Create or align a separate preserved-history model/table for Discord connection state changes without making it the primary active-linkage source.
steps: Add a history table/model for Discord connection/disconnection records.
Keep it separate from the active linkage on profiles.
Use sensible naming and one-table-per-file structure.

items.description: Add focused regression tests covering metadata registration, migrations, and Discord profile/history table behavior.
steps: Add/update tests for metadata table registration and migration presence.
Add/update tests for the profile Discord fields and preserved-history table.

items.description: Run the authoritative docker test suite and mark the spec complete only when green.
steps: Run the full authoritative docker test suite.
Only mark this item complete when the suite is green.
